review_notes: Each score was attacked and survived. Judicial +1/medium for BOTH candidates was the main target — 'restorative justice' (Newsom) and 'the people's court / second chances' (Rothstein) shade toward boilerplate — but the rubric explicitly routes muni-court judicial placement through bail/diversion posture, both candidates have documented diversion advocacy beyond slogans (Newsom: evidence-based diversion with community partners; Rothstein: risk-based release, treatment over punishment), and the identical score for both correctly encodes the dossier's finding that stated philosophy does not differentiate this race — background does. Newsom safety 0/medium is the file's best call: 30 years of prosecution/municipal-government formation genuinely offset by a diversion-and-restorative-justice platform, with no bail position documented — mixed, not unknown. Rothstein safety +2/medium was tested against his victim-safety/accountability language; his stack of evidence (explicit anti-cash-bail-affordability position, drug-treatment-court and recovery-center service, 20-year defense formation) is the deepest in any judicial race here and strictly exceeds evidence that earned +1 elsewhere, so +2 with confidence already discounted to medium stands. Experience split (-1 Newsom with the succession endorsement and three-decade record, 0 Rothstein with pro tem service only since 2024) matches the record. Calkins correctly carries withdrawn=true with no scores. No score relies on the Seattle Times vs. King County Democrats endorsement split.
